sections.paragraph: masc. an inflamed ulcer, a boil (see the root שָׁחַן ) Exo 9:9, 11 -Lev. 13:1820 . שְׁחִין מִצְרַיִם the boil of Egypt, Deu 28:27, 35 Deu 28:35and שְׁחִין רַע Job 2:7 used of a kind of black leprosy endemic in Egypt, called by physicians elephantiasis, from the skin being covered with black scales, and the feet swelling up. Compare Plin. H. N., xxvi. § 5. Schilling, De Lepra, p. 184.
